threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie from a mile away when they try to queue politely at the bus stop. It's a free-for-all here, mate.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy was offering up pizza slices like it was normal. Best midnight snack I've had in ages.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a cat on the U-Bahn platform waiting for the train like it owned the place. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that's also a gallery, serving up art and döner side by side. Truly a feast for the senses!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local by their ability to casually navigate BART delays and still make it to brunch on time, coffee in hand.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a performer on Muni with a two-foot tall parrot. Everyone on the bus was more interested in the bird than their phones.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They always think the local bars are just for tapas. Try asking for a vermut on a Sunday – that's when you'll see the real Gràcia vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they carefully avoid stepping on the cracks in the pavement, slow to realize it's just part of the game here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus is late; real Hackney folk just shrug and check their phones. The Tube strikes are just background noise now.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded us with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall' while the driver just shook his head. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a street musician playing a saxophone while a couple danced in the rain outside the Neukölln Arcaden—pure Berlin moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip your coffee while dodging a spontaneous dance party in the street every Saturday morning. It's wild!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can identify the exact BART delays just by the smell of burnt brakes. It’s an art, really.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy playing the sax on the bus. Everyone was annoyed at first, but by the time he finished, we all clapped. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares and street art, but it’s the hidden pasteleries with line-ups on Sunday mornings that truly define us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered expression at the Sagrada Família's constant construction noise, while locals barely flinch.
